description: When an arrangement recurs clearly enough to be followed or expected, it has a 'sena' (pattern). The repetitions may vary without losing that relation. 'telui' (rhythm) unfolds through time, while 'senalu' (style) gathers characteristic choices across works.
articulatory_notes: Send unvoiced air through a narrow channel at the alveolar ridge for stressed 's' and open into middle-height front 'e'. Move the tongue tip to the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', keep nasal airflow, and finish with open central 'a'.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Art Nouveau patterns often repeat leaves, stems, or curves without making every return identical. 'sena' includes that balance of recurrence and variation.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: recurring arrangement perceived in ornament, sound, movement, or another form
semantic_domains.cognition: structure recognised across repeated or related instances
